title: In-VM activity dispatch and outcome propagation
purpose: Dispatch a Tier-2 in-VM activity as a linked child BEAM process and surface its outcome to the workflow process. This is the in-VM execution path; recording the activity events and deciding retry are seams owned by AD and AT, called from elsewhere.
task: Implement the activity module: dispatch an in-VM activity as a child process linked to the workflow process, choosing the dirty scheduler when the activity's underlying NIF was registered dirty — the dirty flag is looked up from the NIF registration surface (AE-003), which records per (module, function) whether it was registered via register_dirty. Then surface the activity's outcome — a result Payload or an exit signal carrying an ActivityError — back to the workflow process through the link/mailbox. This brief performs no event recording and no retry decision; it provides the dispatch and outcome plumbing those seams build on.

requirements.title: Dispatch as a linked child process
requirements.spec: WHEN an in-VM activity is dispatched, THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn it as a child BEAM process linked to the workflow process via the RuntimeHandle, running on the dirty scheduler when the activity's underlying NIF is flagged dirty.
acceptance: dispatch.rs exposes dispatch_activity(parent_pid, deployed_module, function, input: Payload) -> Result<Pid, EngineError> that calls RuntimeHandle::spawn_activity to spawn-and-link.

The dirty flag is resolved by querying the NIF registration surface (AE-003) for (deployed_module, function): NIFs registered via register_dirty are recorded as dirty there, and dispatch consults that record — it does not guess or carry the flag separately. RuntimeHandle exposes an is_dirty(module, function) -> bool (or equivalent) lookup over the registered NIFs.
When the looked-up flag is dirty, the child runs on beamr's dirty scheduler pool; otherwise on the normal scheduler.
activity/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use.
A test dispatches a trivial in-VM activity from a workflow process and asserts the child is spawned and linked to the parent.

requirements.title: Surface the outcome to the workflow process
requirements.spec: WHEN an activity child completes, THE SYSTEM SHALL surface its result Payload to the workflow process; WHEN it exits abnormally, THE SYSTEM SHALL surface an exit signal carrying an ActivityError to the workflow process through the link/mailbox.
acceptance: On success, the activity's result is delivered to the workflow process as a result Payload (via the mailbox / linked completion path).
On failure, the workflow process receives a trapped-exit message whose reason carries an aion_core ActivityError (preserving the retryable/terminal classification).

A test dispatches an activity that returns a value and asserts the workflow process receives the result Payload; a second test dispatches one that fails and asserts the workflow process receives an ActivityError-bearing exit.

requirements.title: No recording, no retry decision here
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT append any Activity* event and SHALL NOT make any retry decision in the dispatch path; recording is delegated to the AD append path and the retry decision to AT's policy machinery.
acceptance: dispatch.rs contains no call that appends ActivityScheduled/Started/Completed/Failed events and no RetryPolicy evaluation.
Module docs state that recording is AD's append path and retry is AT's machinery, and that dispatch only spawns, links, and propagates the outcome.
grep of activity/ shows no store append call and no retry-loop construct.

boundaries: SHALL NOT record Activity* events — that is the AD append path.
SHALL NOT evaluate or apply RetryPolicy — that is AT's activity machinery, which consumes the ActivityError this brief surfaces.
SHALL NOT dispatch remote (Tier-3) activities — those ride aion-server's worker protocol (AW); this is the in-VM path only.
SHALL NOT set trap-exit or build the supervision tree — that is AE-006, on which this brief depends.
verification: Dispatch path performs no event recording and no retry decision (D7).
ActivityError's retryable/terminal classification is preserved through the exit reason.
